title: A Charity Helps Save a River
summary: A charity called Severn Rivers Trust wants to help the River Teme. From April 22 to 29, they will match all donations people give. This means if you give money, they add the same amount. The money will be used to fix the river, make the water cleaner, protect animals and plants, and stop floods. The river has problems like pollution, not enough trees, and banks that are falling apart. The charity works with farmers and others to plant trees, make ponds, and help fish like Atlantic salmon swim better. They say everyone can help by donating, and it will make a big difference for the river.

keywords.explanation: a group that helps people or the environment without making money
money or gifts given to help a cause
fixing a river to make it healthy again
dirty things in the water or air that can harm nature
a type of fish that lives in rivers and needs clean water to lay eggs

Who does the charity work with to help the river?
What fish will benefit from the charity's work?

background_read: The River Teme is a river in England that needs help because it is dirty and damaged. Charities like Severn Rivers Trust work to clean rivers by planting trees, making ponds, and fixing banks. This helps fish and other wildlife live better. Donations from people can double during special times, making more money for these projects. Farmers and local groups often help too, showing that everyone can work together to protect nature.
Article_Structure: WHO: Severn Rivers Trust, a charity, and people who donate money.
WHAT: Matching donations to raise money for fixing the River Teme, improving water, protecting animals, and stopping floods.
WHERE: The River Teme in England.
WHY: Because the river has problems like pollution and erosion, and helping it will make it healthier for fish and plants.

perspectives.description: The charity believes that by raising money and working with others, they can fix the river and protect nature for the future.
